🎬↔📋 Protocol–Video Alignment — Claude vs VLM (our own demo)

Self-collected demo: IMG_2046.MOV (PCR mutagenesis setup, 156s), 24 frames, reviewed by Claude · claude-opus-4-8 and Qwen2.5-VL-3B-Instruct side by side with a Claude↔VLM agreement strip. No ground-truth timestamps were collected, so this is a qualitative comparison (not scored vs truth).

1. Thaw Q5 Hot Start Master Mix, forward primer, reverse primer, plasmid template, and nuclease-free water.
2. Label 2 PCR tubes (Negative Control, Mutagenesis)
3. Prepare the PCR master mix for 2 reactions (12 µL Q5 Hot Start Master Mix, 2 µL Forward Primer (10 µM), 2 µL Reverse Primer (10 µM), 2 µL nuclease-free water)
4. Mix the master mix gently until homogeneous.
5. Add 6 µL master mix to each PCR tube.
6. Add to Mutagenesis 2 µL plasmid template
7. Add to Negative Control 2 µL nuclease-free water
8. Gently pipette-mix each reaction.
9. Briefly spin tubes to collect liquid at the bottom.
10. Place tubes into the thermocycler.

9. Briefly spin tubes to collect liquid at the bottom.likely done

10. Place tubes into the thermocycler.likely done
⏱ 2m25s – 2m36s
A thermocycler with open lid and active touchscreen is operated, consistent with loading tubes and starting the run.
What it observed
A researcher in a lab coat and blue gloves works at a crowded bench with multimchannel pipettes overhead, yellow tube racks holding small labeled tubes, a reagent bottle, tip boxes, and a notebook. Early frames (0-2) show them holding a marker and appearing to label/write near the tubes. Frames 3-24 show extended single-channel pipetting activity: drawing liquid, holding small tubes, dispensing into tubes in the yellow racks, and changing tips repeatedly. Around frame 24 pipettes are set down. Frames 25-26 show tubes being placed/handled in a small benchtop microcentrifuge (myFUGE). Frames 27-28 show a thermocycler with its lid open and the touchscreen being operated. The final frame (29) shows the researcher smiling with a thumbs-up. Specific reagent identities and exact volumes are not legible from the frames.
